chapter_context.summary: The Israelites worship a golden calf; Moses breaks the stone tablets and intercedes for the people.

outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Golden Calf (32:1-6), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Exodus.
This section focuses on Moses Intercedes (32:7-14), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Exodus.
This section focuses on The Tablets Broken (32:15-24), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Exodus.
This section focuses on Judgment and Consequence (32:25-35), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Exodus.

key_themes.title: Deliverance
key_themes.description: God's power in rescuing His people from bondage.

key_themes.title: Covenant Presence
key_themes.description: God dwelling among His people in the Tabernacle.

devotional.question: How can we 'stand still and see the salvation of the LORD' in our lives today?
What does the detail of the Tabernacle teach us about God's holiness and proximity?

cultural_historical.title: The Name of God
cultural_historical.text: The revelation of 'YHWH' (I AM) marks a turning point in God's relationship with Israel, signifying His self-existence and faithfulness.
